name: Fenofibric acid
description: An antilipemic agent which reduces both cholesterol and triglycerides in the blood.
indication: For use as adjunctive therapy to diet to reduce elevated LDL-C, Total-C,Triglycerides and Apo B, and to increase HDL-C in adult patients with primary hypercholesterolemia or mixed dyslipidemia (Fredrickson Types IIa and IIb).

interactions.name: Ciprofibrate
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Fenofibric acid is combined with Ciprofibrate.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Cyclosporine
interactions.description: Cyclosporine may increase the nephrotoxic activities of Fenofibric acid.

interactions.name: Colestipol
interactions.description: Colestipol can cause a decrease in the absorption of Fenofibric acid resulting in a reduced serum concentration and potentially a decrease in efficacy.

interactions.name: Colesevelam
interactions.description: Colesevelam can cause a decrease in the absorption of Fenofibric acid resulting in a reduced serum concentration and potentially a decrease in efficacy.

interactions.name: Cholestyramine
interactions.description: Cholestyramine can cause a decrease in the absorption of Fenofibric acid resulting in a reduced serum concentration and potentially a decrease in efficacy.

interactions.name: Chlorpropamide
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Chlorpropamide.

interactions.name: Tolazamide
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Tolazamide.

interactions.name: Glyburide
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Glyburide.

interactions.name: Glipizide
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Glipizide.

interactions.name: Gliclazide
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Gliclazide.

interactions.name: Glimepiride
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Glimepiride.

interactions.name: Tolbutamide
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Tolbutamide.

interactions.name: Acetohexamide
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Acetohexamide.

interactions.name: Gliquidone
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Gliquidone.

interactions.name: Glisoxepide
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Glisoxepide.

interactions.name: Glibornuride
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Glibornuride.

interactions.name: Carbutamide
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Carbutamide.

interactions.name: Metahexamide
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the hypoglycemic activities of Metahexamide.

interactions.name: Acipimox
interactions.description: Acipimox may increase the myopathic rhabdomyolysis activities of Fenofibric acid.

interactions.name: Colchicine
interactions.description: Fenofibric acid may increase the myopathic rhabdomyolysis activities of Colchicine.

interactions.name: Raltegravir
interactions.description: Raltegravir may increase the myopathic rhabdomyolysis activities of Fenofibric acid.

interactions.name: Chenodeoxycholic acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Chenodeoxycholic acid can be decreased when used in combination with Fenofibric acid.

interactions.name: Ursodeoxycholic acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Ursodeoxycholic acid can be decreased when used in combination with Fenofibric acid.
